step.title: Validate & Complete
step.goal: Validate the completed agent source files, update the manifest, run the build, verify the compiled artifact, and present next steps.

substep.title: Verify File Completeness
substep.action:
Check that all expected files exist in .vera/agents/{agent_id}/:
Primary agent: - agent.yaml (artifact descriptor with type/id/file_name/variables/includes) - frontmatter.md (YAML frontmatter as raw content) - persona.md (<persona> block) - menu.md (<menu> block)
Subagent: - agent.yaml (artifact descriptor) - frontmatter.md (YAML frontmatter with mode: subagent) - instructions.md (subagent body)
For each file, verify: - No remaining FILL comments (all sections filled) - No TODO markers (all values set) - Valid YAML syntax (for agent.yaml) - Valid XML structure inside persona.md / menu.md / instructions.md - frontmatter.md begins and ends with `---` lines

substep.title: Cross-Reference Validation
substep.action:
Verify consistency across files:
1. Every skill in frontmatter.md permission map has a corresponding menu item in menu.md (primary agents) 2. Every menu item's skill="..." attribute matches a skill ID that exists under .vera/skills/{skill-id}/ (or is on the create list for this session) 3. agent.yaml includes list matches the composition agreed in Step 2 4. agent.yaml variables block has agent_id/character_name/agent_title/emoji (primary agents) 5. agent.yaml id matches the directory name and {file_name}.md basename 6. shared/ paths in includes resolve to actual files under .vera/shared/
Run the validator script: uv run .vera/skills/agent-creator/references/scripts/validate-agent.py .vera/agents/{agent_id}/

substep.title: Update vera.manifest.yaml
substep.action:
Add the agent's artifact entry to the appropriate build group in .vera/vera.manifest.yaml. Existing build groups: - ticketsmith — ticket-writing workflow - codesmith — implementation workflow - prompter — agent-authoring meta-workflow
Pick the existing build group if the agent extends one of these workflows. Create a new build group if the agent is for a new workflow domain.
Entry format: - agents/{agent_id}/agent.yaml
For new build groups: {build_group_name}: artifacts: - agents/{agent_id}/agent.yaml - skills/{skill-1}/skill.yaml ...

substep.title: Run Build
substep.action:
Run: vera build
vera-cli compiles ALL artifacts listed in .vera/vera.manifest.yaml. There is no per-agent build subcommand in this version — the manifest is the build unit.
If build succeeds: - Verify the compiled artifact at the configured output path (typically ~/.config/opencode/agent/{agent_id}.md per .vera/vera.config.yaml) - Check that {{variables}} were substituted (agent_id, character_name, agent_title, emoji, user_name, communication_language all resolved to literal values) - Spot-check the <agent> open tag has all four identity attributes - Spot-check the menu items appear in the right order
If build fails: - Read the error messages - Common issues: missing include file path, wrong shared/ reference, malformed YAML in agent.yaml or frontmatter.md, unresolved {{variable}} - Fix and re-run vera build

substep.title: Present Summary
substep.template-output:
## Agent Created Successfully
**Source location:** `.vera/agents/{agent_id}/` **Compiled artifact:** `~/.config/opencode/agent/{agent_id}.md` (or per vera.config.yaml output_dir)
**Files:** - agent.yaml (artifact descriptor) - frontmatter.md - (persona.md + menu.md for primary, OR instructions.md for subagent)
**Identity:** - ID: {agent_id} - Name: {character_name} - Title: {agent_title} - Emoji: {emoji}
**Skills:** (list from Step 1) **Build group in manifest:** {build_group_name}
**Next Steps:** 1. Create each skill using [CS] Create Skill (skills referenced in menu.md must exist under .vera/skills/ for the agent to function) 2. Re-run `vera build` after each skill addition 3. Test the agent in OpenCode by selecting it from the agent picker
**Note:** The agent compiles cleanly even if its skills don't exist yet — but invoking missing skills at runtime will surface errors. Build the dependencies before testing end-to-end.

rules.rule: Missing include file → verify path under .vera/ resolves (shared/ paths must exist at .vera/shared/...)
YAML syntax error → check indentation, quotes, special characters in agent.yaml or frontmatter.md
Unresolved {{variable}} → add to agent.yaml variables block OR confirm 4-layer merge picks it up (user_name and communication_language come from ~/.config/vera/config.yaml)
Wrong frontmatter location → frontmatter MUST be raw markdown content in frontmatter.md, NOT nested YAML in agent.yaml (that's the legacy format)

section.title: What Comes Next
section.description:
Creating the agent is step 1. The agent needs skills to be useful. Each skill identified in Step 1 should be created using the skill-creator workflow [CS]. Skills define the agent's actual capabilities — without them, the agent is just a persona with an empty menu.
After all skills are created and the manifest is complete, run `vera build` once more to compile the full set. Then test in OpenCode end-to-end: greeting, menu presentation, skill invocation, completion handoff.
